name: family-presentation-wiki
purpose: Generate a person-first family-history presentation layer from canonical genealogy wiki pages, while keeping research dashboards, converted chunks, QA, staging, and source operations in the research backroom.
model: Karpathy-style durable Markdown/JSON wiki state feeding an internal Ancestry-style browsing experience.
public_navigation_contract: Home shows the home person, family tree preview, people, relationships, family lines, LifeStory chapters, and family timeline.
Tree shows canonical people plus reviewed relationship evidence, not source clusters.
People shows canonical person profiles and their relationship to the home person.
Timeline shows family events and person facts only; source dates and automation dates stay out of the presentation timeline.
Sources are supporting citations and source-library material, not the main narrative.
Research shows QA, queues, staging, proof review, source usability, and automation state.

automatic_update_path.promotion_gate: Scheduled runs use a separate wiki_promoter pass with allow_promotion=true; promotion still requires reviewed canonical_readiness=ready. Promotion refreshes person evidence snapshots from canonical claims and relationships so stub profiles become populated automatically.
triggers: push to main touching wiki, research, or site builder code

separation_rules: Do not put conversion chunks, queue prompts, or QA internals on the public home page.
Do not let converted source dates populate the public family timeline.
Do not turn a source cluster into a family story until it is attached to a person, event, place, relationship, photo, or narrative.
Keep research confidence, source quality, conversion confidence, relevance, and claim probability visible where useful, but do not reduce the family view to promoted/not-promoted.
Family-supplied knowledge may seed canonical pages and relationships, with later records attached as support rather than replacing the person-first structure.
